question_title: Which conference will the NCAA Division I men’s basketball national champion belong to?
summary: The 2026 NCAA tournament championship odds are dominated by four conferences. Duke (ACC) and Michigan (Big Ten) are co-favorites at +325, followed by Arizona (Big 12) at +475 and Florida (SEC) at +590. Big Ten and Big 12 have the most depth of strong contenders. I'm anchoring directly on the market-implied probabilities from multiple sportsbooks (OddsShark, bet365, DraftKings, FanDuel) as of March 14, 2026, which represent the best available aggregation of expert analysis and financial stakes. The #1 seeds are Duke (ACC), Michigan (Big Ten), Iowa State (Big 12), and Houston (Big 12), giving Big 12 two of four #1 seeds. "Other" is primarily driven by UConn (Big East) at +1700.

factors.description: Duke (ACC) is co-favorite at +325 on OddsShark (March 14, 2026), the strongest single-team asset for ACC
Michigan (Big Ten) is co-favorite at +325, the strongest single-team asset for Big Ten
Arizona (Big 12) is #3 favorite at +475, plus Big 12 has depth with Houston (+1100), Iowa State (+2500), Kansas (+4500) - and 2 of 4 #1 seeds are Big 12
Florida (SEC) is #4 at +590, but SEC lacks depth behind Florida - Auburn (+4000), Tennessee (+5000), Alabama (+8000) are much longer odds
UConn (+1700) is the only realistic 'Other' conference contender; Big East/WCC/mid-majors all have very long odds
Big Ten has more depth behind Michigan: Illinois (+2200), Purdue (+3500), Michigan State (+4000) supplement the favorite
ACC has less depth behind Duke: Louisville (+3000) and North Carolina (+5000) are secondary contributors with longer odds

reasoning:
**Forecast: 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Champion by Conference**

| Conference | Probability |
|------------|------------|
| Big Ten | 27% |
| Big 12 | 25% |
| ACC | 22% |
| SEC | 16% |
| Other | 10% |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Big Ten (27%)**: Led by Michigan (+325 co-favorite), with depth from Illinois, Purdue, and Michigan State
- **Big 12 (25%)**: Arizona (+475) is #3 favorite, plus Houston and Iowa State give the conference 2 of 4 #1 seeds
- **ACC (22%)**: Duke (+325 co-favorite) carries the conference but it's top-heavy
- **SEC (16%)**: Florida (+590) is the #4 favorite but SEC lacks depth of contenders
- **Other (10%)**: UConn (+1700) from the Big East is the primary driver; mid-major auto-bids contribute marginally

These probabilities closely track the market-implied distribution from current sportsbook odds as of March 14, 2026.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option that matches the conference affiliation of the 2026 NCAA Division I men’s basketball champion shown on the official NCAA tournament bracket and results page at https://www.ncaa.com/news/basketball-men/mml-official-bracket/2026-01-28/2026-ncaa-tournament-printable-bracket-schedule-march-madness
fine_print: Use the conference affiliation recognized by NCAA for the champion's school in the 2025–26 season. If the school is independent or not in a listed option, resolve to "Other." Candidate Sources: NCAA.com men's basketball pages linked from the official bracket page.
